title: Low-Data Learning Path
description: Build fluency in Low-Data Learning inside Learning Paradigms by moving from foundational terms into adjacent methods, variants, and extensions.

steps.title: Few-shot Learning
steps.summary: The ability of a model to learn from a small number of examples. It sits inside Learning Paradigms / Low-Data Learning. Start after Q-Learning and V-Learning. It opens into Deep Few-Shot Learning and Few-Shot Meta-Learning.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: One-shot Learning
steps.summary: One-shot Learning belongs to the Learning Paradigms area of AI/ML, within Low-Data Learning. It sits inside Learning Paradigms / Low-Data Learning. Start after Q-Learning and V-Learning. It opens into One-Shot Imitation Learning and One-Shot Learning Techniques.
steps.whyIncluded: Start here to anchor the vocabulary and core intuition for this area.

steps.title: Zero-shot Learning
steps.summary: Zero-shot Learning belongs to the Learning Paradigms area of AI/ML, within Low-Data Learning. It sits inside Learning Paradigms / Low-Data Learning. Start after Zero-1-to-3 and Q-Learning. It opens into Zero-Shot Meta-Learning and Zero-Shot Transfer Learning.
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Zero-Shot Neural Machine Translation
steps.summary: Zero-Shot Neural Machine Translation belongs to the Learning Paradigms area of AI/ML, within Low-Data Learning. It sits inside Learning Paradigms / Low-Data Learning. Start after Zero-Shot Translation and Zero-1-to-3. It opens into Zero-Shot Neural Machine Translation (NMT) and Zero-Shot Neural Machine Translation (NMT) Extensions.
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Zero-Shot Neural Machine Translation (NMT)
steps.summary: Zero-Shot Neural Machine Translation (NMT) belongs to the Learning Paradigms area of AI/ML, within Low-Data Learning. It sits inside Learning Paradigms / Low-Data Learning. Start after Zero-Shot Neural Machine Translation and Zero-Shot Translation. It opens into Zero-Shot Neural Machine Translation (NMT) Extensions and Zero-Shot Neural Machine Translation (NMT) Techniques.
steps.whyIncluded: Use this after the foundations to connect patterns, variants, and system behavior.

steps.title: Zero-Shot Classification
steps.summary: Zero-Shot Classification belongs to the Learning Paradigms area of AI/ML, within Low-Data Learning. It sits inside Learning Paradigms / Low-Data Learning. Start after Zero-1-to-3 and Classification. It opens into Zero-Shot Text Classification and Zero-Shot Image Classification.
steps.whyIncluded: Treat this as an advanced extension once the earlier terms feel natural.

steps.title: Leave-one-out cross validation
steps.summary: Leave-one-out cross validation belongs to the Learning Paradigms area of AI/ML, within Low-Data Learning. It sits inside Learning Paradigms / Low-Data Learning. Start after Leave-P-Out Cross-Validation and Cross-validation. It opens into Leave-One-Out Cross-Validation (LOOCV).
steps.whyIncluded: Treat this as an advanced extension once the earlier terms feel natural.

steps.title: Zero-Inflated Models
steps.summary: Zero-Inflated Models belongs to the Learning Paradigms area of AI/ML, within Low-Data Learning. It sits inside Learning Paradigms / Low-Data Learning. Start after Zero-1-to-3. It opens into Zero-Inflated Regression and Hurdle/Zero-Inflated GBMs.
steps.whyIncluded: Treat this as an advanced extension once the earlier terms feel natural.
